utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi , ms. thompson . i'm dr. moore . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hi , dr. moore .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing okay except for my knee .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , hey , dragon , ms. thompson is a 43 year old female here for right knee pain . so tell me what happened with your knee ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , i was , um , trying to change a light bulb , and i was up on a ladder and i kinda had a little bit of a stumble and kinda twisted my knee as i was trying to catch my fall .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and did you injure yourself any place else ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , no . it just seems to be the knee .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . and when did this happen ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it was yesterday .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . and , uh , where does it hurt mostly ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it hurts like in , in , in the inside of my knee .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: right here .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . and anything make it better or worse ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have been putting ice on it , uh , and i've been taking ibuprofen , but it does n't seem to help much .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so it sounds like you fell a couple days ago , and you've hurt something inside of your right knee .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and you've been taking a little bit of ice , uh , putting some ice on it , and has n't really helped and some ibuprofen . is that right ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's right . yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , let's review your past history for a second . it looks like , uh , do you have any other past medical history ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , afib .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and are you taking any medications for that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i am . um , begins with a d.

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: uh , digoxin ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's it . yeah , that's it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . how about any surgeries in the past ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have had a nose job .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . um , let's do your exam , okay ? so is it tender ... where is it mostly tender right now ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: right on the inside of my knee . right here .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , so if i bend your knee forward , does that seem to hurt ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , that hurts .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , how about if i twist it a little bit that way .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that hurts a lot .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , okay . and how about down here ? do you feel me touch you down here ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . any other pain down here in your calves ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no , okay . so on exam you do have some tenderness over the medial portion of your knee over the medial meniscus area . uh , there is no , uh , there is a little bit of tenderness when i flex your , uh , when i , uh , uh , do some valgus stressing on your , on your leg . um , you have normal sensation . so let's take a look at your x-rays .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . hey dragon , show me the x-rays . so looking at the x-ray , um , of your left knee , uh , it appears to be there's no fractures there right now . i do n't see any , uh , there's a little bit of , uh , fluid , uh , but there is no , uh , there's no , um , fracture or there's no dislocation . everything else seems to be lined up properly , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so in summary after my exam , uh , looking at your knee , uh , on the x-ray and your exam , you have some tenderness over the medial meniscus , so i think you have probably an acute medial meniscus sprain right now or strain . uh , at this point , my recommendation would be to put you in a knee brace , uh , and we'll go ahead and have you use some crutches temporarily for the next couple days . we'll have you come back in about a week and see how you're doing , and if it's not better , we'll get an mri at that time .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm going to recommend we give you some motrin , 800 milligrams . uh , you can take it about every six hours , uh , with food . uh , and we'll give you about a two week supply .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . uh , do you have any questions ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i think i'm good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . hey , dragon , order the medications and procedures discussed , and finalize the report . okay , come with me and we'll get you checked out .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 43-year-old female presenting for evaluation of right knee pain.
The patient twisted her right knee while stumbling on a ladder yesterday.
The doctor asks if the patient has any other injuries.
The patient denies any other injuries.
The doctor asks when the injury occurred.
The injury occurred yesterday.
The doctor asks for the location of the pain.
The patient reports pain on the inside of the right knee.
The doctor asks about factors that improve or worsen the pain.
The patient has been using ice and ibuprofen with little relief.
Review of past medical history.
The doctor asks about the patient's past medical history.
The patient has a history of atrial fibrillation.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking medication for atrial fibrillation.
The patient is taking digoxin.
The doctor asks about past surgical history.
The patient has a history of rhinoplasty.
Physical examination.
The doctor performs flexion of the knee.
The doctor performs valgus stress testing on the knee.
The doctor assesses sensation in the lower leg.
The doctor asks if there is pain in the calves.
The patient denies calf pain.
Exam shows medial joint line tenderness, pain with flexion and valgus stress, and intact sensation.
X-rays show no fracture or dislocation, but mild effusion is present.
The diagnosis is an acute medial meniscus sprain.
The patient will use a knee brace and crutches for a few days, follow up in one week, and potentially obtain an MRI if not improved.
The patient is prescribed Motrin 800 mg every 6 hours with food for two weeks.
The doctor asks if the patient has any questions.

case_description: right knee pain; atrial fibrillation;hx rhinoplasty
